subject: [PATCH] mm/vmpressure: skip socket pressure for costly order reclaim

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Identified that reclaim efficiency is misleading at high orders and suggested removing socket pressure in such cases. Proposed adding a comment to explain this reasoning.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, Apr 01, 2026 at 01:37:52PM -0700, JP Kobryn (Meta) wrote:
> @@ -307,7 +308,7 @@ void vmpressure(gfp_t gfp, struct mem_cgroup *memcg, bool tree,
>  
>  		level = vmpressure_calc_level(scanned, reclaimed);
>  
> -		if (level > VMPRESSURE_LOW) {
> +		if (level > VMPRESSURE_LOW && order <= PAGE_ALLOC_COSTLY_ORDER) {

I think a comment would be in, ahem, order.

Once we go above COSTLY_ORDER, reclaim relies heavily on compaction to
make progress. Reclaim efficiency was never a great proxy for pressure
to begin with, but it's outright misleading with these high
orders. Don't throttle sockets because somebody is attempting
something crazy like an order-7 and predictably struggling.

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: Suggested dropping the 'extern' keyword while modifying a function declaration.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, Apr 01, 2026 at 01:37:52PM -0700, JP Kobryn (Meta) wrote:
>  #ifdef CONFIG_MEMCG
> -extern void vmpressure(gfp_t gfp, struct mem_cgroup *memcg, bool tree,
> -		       unsigned long scanned, unsigned long reclaimed);
> +extern void vmpressure(gfp_t gfp, int order, struct mem_cgroup *memcg,
> +		       bool tree, unsigned long scanned, unsigned long reclaimed);

You can drop the 'extern' while you're changing this line.

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch modifies the vmpressure() function to take an additional 'order' parameter, sourced from scan_control. It then uses this order to filter out socket pressure when reclaiming at high orders (above PAGE_ALLOC_COSTLY_ORDER). This change aims to prevent unnecessary throttling of TCP throughput due to poor reclaim efficiency.
